results.notes: collects_from_mds failed due to subagent bash sandbox, not skill issue. New layer1_only assertion passed — only 4 facts in preview table.
5 assertions failed because bash was denied and skill correctly stopped. The 1 pass (respects_no_fallback) validates the 'do not substitute' instruction works.

observations: Subagent bash sandbox is the dominant confounder — both configs could not run the collector. With-skill correctly stops; without-skill falls back to static analysis.
Test 1 with-skill shows the layered approach working: only 4 critical facts in preview table (layer1_only passed). Iteration 1 had 4 tables + 5 concerns.
Test 2 unchanged: with-skill asks 2 focused questions (3/3), baseline dumps data (0/3).
Test 3 with-skill scored 1/6 but 5 failures are all bash-denied cascading from collection step. The 1 pass (respects_no_fallback) validates the new strict instruction.
With-skill used 27% fewer tokens and was 56% faster on average — largely because it stops early when collection fails rather than falling back to extensive static analysis.
To properly evaluate Tests 1 and 3, we need to test in the main conversation where bash works, or grant subagent bash permissions.
